e	y^
This module provides an interface to the native time zone data on Windows,
including :py:class:`datetime.tzinfo` implementations.
Attempting to import this module on a non-Windows platform will raise an
:py:obj:`ImportError`.
winreg)
	text_type)
wintypesz#Running tzwin on non-Windows system

3/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/dateutil/tz/win.py
_settzkeyname"
    Class for accessing ``tzres.dll``, which contains timezone name related
    resources.
    .. versionadded:: 2.5.0
    
	tzres.dllc
user32)
ctypesZ

Z	HINSTANCEZ

_tzres
	tzres_loc)
selfr
__init__9
tzres.__init__c
        Load a timezone name from a DLL offset (integer).
        >>> from dateutil.tzwin import tzres
        >>> tzr = tzres()
        >>> print(tzr.load_name(112))
        'Eastern Standard Time'
        :param offset:
            A positive integer value referring to a string from the tzres dll.
        .. note::
            Offsets found in the registry are generally of the form
            ``@tzres.dll,-114``. The offset in this case is 114, not -114.
        r
p_wcharr
castZ
byrefr
_handle)
offset
resourceZ
lpBufferZ
ncharr
	load_nameG
tzres.load_namec
        Parse strings as returned from the Windows registry into the time zone
        name as defined in the registry.
        >>> from dateutil.tzwin import tzres
        >>> tzr = tzres()
        >>> print(tzr.name_from_string('@tzres.dll,-251'))
        'Dateline Daylight Time'
        >>> print(tzr.name_from_string('Eastern Standard Time'))
        'Eastern Standard Time'
        :param tzname_str:
            A timezone name string as returned from a Windows registry key.
        :return:
            Returns the localized timezone string from tzres.dll if the string
            is of the form `@tzres.dll,-offset`, else returns the input string.
        
Malformed timezone string.)

tzwinbase.listc
        Return the display name of the time zone.
        )
_displayr1
display
tzwinbase.displayc
        For a given year, get the DST on and off transition times, expressed
        always on the standard time side. For zones with no transitions, this
        function returns ``None``.
        :param year:
            The year whose transitions you would like to query.
        :return:
            Returns a :class:`tuple` of :class:`datetime.datetime` objects,
            ``(dston, dstoff)`` for zones with an annual DST transition, or
            ``None`` for fixed offset zones.
        N)
hasdst
picknthweekday
	_dstmonthr7
	_stdmonthr6
_dst_base_offset)
yearZ
dstonZ

transitions
tzwinbase.transitionsc
_get_hasdst
tzwinbase._get_hasdstc
_dst_base_offset_r1
tzwinbase._dst_base_offsetN)
staticmethodrJ
propertyrQ
    Time zone object created from the zone info in the Windows registry
    These are similar to :py:class:`dateutil.tz.tzrange` objects in that
    the time zone data is provided in the format of a single offset rule
    for either 0 or 2 time zone transitions per year.
    :param: name
        The name of a Windows time zone key, e.g. "Eastern Standard Time".
        The full list of keys can be retrieved with :func:`tzwin.list`.
    c
{kn}\{name})
nameZ
StdZ
Displayz
=3l16hZ
TZIr
minutes
_namer
formatr
valuestodictr>
struct
unpack
datetime
	timedeltar4
	tzkeynamerE
keydict
	stdoffset
	dstoffsetr
tzwin.__init__c
Nz	tzwin(%s))
reprra
__repr__
tzwin.__repr__c
	__class__ra
__reduce__
tzwin.__reduce__N)
    Class representing the local time zone information in the Windows registry
    While :class:`dateutil.tz.tzlocal` makes system calls (via the :mod:`time`
    module) to retrieve time zone information, ``tzwinlocal`` retrieves the
    rules directly from the Windows registry and creates an object like
    :class:`dateutil.tz.tzwin`.
    Because Windows does not have an equivalent of :func:`time.tzset`, on
    Windows, :class:`dateutil.tz.tzlocal` instances will always reflect the
    time zone settings *at the time that the process was started*, meaning
    changes to the machine's time zone settings during the run of a program
    on Windows will **not** be reflected by :class:`dateutil.tz.tzlocal`.
    Because ``tzwinlocal`` reads the registry directly, it is unaffected by
    this issue.
    c
}	|	d
}	|	d
StandardNameZ
DaylightNamez	{kn}\{sn})

BiasZ
StandardBiasZ
DaylightBiasr\

z> dayofweek == 0 means Sunday, whichweek 5 means last instance r
